answer.text: The magnitude of expected price swings
The expected range of price movement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** IV indicates expected magnitude of price swings but NOT direction. High IV means large price swings (up OR down). IV cannot tell you whether the price will go up or down. **The Trap:** IV is about the SIZE of expected moves, not their direction. **Cross-Exam Context:** IV interpretation is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A 10% IV on an annual basis means the market expects the underlying to move about ±10% over a year (roughly ±2.9% per month).
